text: BREAKING: Iran's Hormuz reopening proposal REJECTED by Rubio. He insists any deal must address nuclear program. Trump reviewed with NSC but reportedly 'unhappy' — said Iran 'offered a lot, but not enough.' Diplomacy stalled again. Brent surging to $110+.
verificationNote: CNBC, Foreign Policy, Times of Israel. Confirmed.

text: SECOND Hezbollah drone strike wounds IDF soldiers in southern Lebanon buffer zone — 1 severely, 1 lightly. Follows killing of Sgt. Idan Fooks (19) on Apr 26. Hezbollah now 100+ rockets since ceasefire, ~10,000 projectiles since Feb 28. Netanyahu: Hezbollah 'dismantling the ceasefire.'
verificationNote: Times of Israel liveblog, Haaretz, Euronews. All confirmed.

text: German Chancellor Merz publicly says US 'being humiliated' by Iran — 'clearly stronger than one thought.' Deepest transatlantic rift of the war. Meanwhile Guterres appeals: 'Open the Strait. No tolls. No discrimination.' Warns of global food emergency — 1/3 of fertilizer trade transits Hormuz.
verificationNote: Al Jazeera, The Hill, Bloomberg (Merz). UN News, Deccan Chronicle (Guterres). Confirmed.

text: MARKETS DAY 60: Brent $110.44 (+38.3% from pre-war $79.84). WTI $97.32 (+28.9%). IEA calls it 'largest energy supply disruption in history' — 10.1 mb/d plunge. EIA: Brent spot-futures spread at RECORD $25+ (extreme backwardation). Goldman Q4 Brent at $90 (raised from $80). S&P 500 at record highs despite war — AI/tech decoupled.
verificationNote: Trading Economics, IEA, EIA, Benzinga/Goldman, CNBC. All confirmed.

text: WAR POWERS UPDATE: Senate Republicans defeated Iran War Powers resolution for 5TH time on Apr 23 (46-51). Only Rand Paul (R) crossed. Fetterman (D) voted against. May 1 deadline in 3 days. Veterans anti-war protest on Capitol Hill — 62 arrested at Cannon House Office Building incl. disabled activists.
verificationNote: Democracy Now, Time, Foreign Policy, OPB. Confirmed.

text: DAY 60 LATE PM PT ROUNDUP: Iran Hormuz offer REJECTED by Rubio. Trump 'unhappy.' Brent $110.44 (+38.3%). WTI $97.32. Gas ~$4.06 AAA. Guterres: 'Open the Strait.' Merz: US 'humiliated.' 2nd Hezbollah drone wounds IDF. Pezeshkian: 'will not negotiate under pressure.' Qassem rejects direct talks. War Powers 5th defeat. Veterans 62 arrested. Hormuz Day 57. May 1 (3 days).

text: GCC EXTRAORDINARY SUMMIT: Gulf states convene in Jeddah — joint statement demands "urgent and unimpeded reopening" of Hormuz. Qatar's Emir Tamim warns against "frozen conflict" — says Hormuz must not be used as "pressure card." Bahrain's King Hamad leads call. UAE FM Abdullah bin Zayed present. Unified Gulf front forming against both blockades.

KALLAS STATEMENT: EU High Rep at EU-ASEAN Brunei warns Hormuz blockade sets "dangerous precedence" for global freedom of navigation: "Freedom of navigation must remain free or it will set dangerous precedence elsewhere in the world." Widening international chorus demanding reopening. Insurance Journal reports 2-6% war risk premiums (from 0.25% pre-war, 1000-2400% increase).

ZELENSKY-ISRAEL DISPUTE: Ukraine threatens sanctions on Israel over Russian ships carrying "stolen" Ukrainian grain docking at Haifa port. Ukraine summons Israeli ambassador. Israeli FM Sa'ar: "no evidence." Unprecedented wartime diplomatic rift between two countries both facing existential threats. US State Dept silent on the dispute.
verificationNote: Al Jazeera, Kyiv Independent, Times of Israel. All confirmed.

text: BRENT SURGES TO $111.16 (+2.71% daily, +39.3% from pre-war $79.84). WTI $99.43 (+31.7%). Oil rallies on continued stalemate over rejected Hormuz proposal. IMF downgraded global growth to 3.1% (from 3.3%). Iran GDP contraction -6.1%. Iran oil storage crisis: 12-22 days capacity remaining — may force 1.5M bpd production cut by mid-May.

text: DAY 60 MIDDAY PT ROUNDUP: GCC emergency summit demands Hormuz reopening. Kallas: "dangerous precedence." Zelensky threatens Israel sanctions over Russian grain. US $10M bounty on 3rd Iraqi militia leader Al Gharawi. Brent $111.16 (+39.3%). WTI $99.43. Gas $4.031 AAA. Lebanon DT 2,509 / 7,750+ wounded. Qassem rejects direct talks. Hormuz Day 57. text: TRUMP TRUTH SOCIAL: Posts that Iran has 'just informed us they are in a State of Collapse' and 'want us to Open the Hormuz Strait.' Posted within hours of Rubio's 'economic nuclear weapon' framing of Iran's Hormuz tactic.
verificationNote: Fox News live coverage of Truth Social posts. Confirmed.

text: IDF DAY 60 STRIKE PACKAGE: ~20 Hezbollah targets struck across Beqaa Valley and southern Lebanon today — biggest wave since April 16 ceasefire extension. IDF announces destruction of two ~2km cross-border attack tunnels in Qantara, ~25m deep, says built with 'direct guidance from Iran' over a decade. Some northern Israeli towns canceled school. Netanyahu: Hezbollah 'trying to derail talks.'
verificationNote: Times of Israel liveblog, Jerusalem Post live updates. Confirmed.

Urges US-Iran return to negotiating table. Vatican joins UN, EU, GCC, and Pakistan in growing chorus of mediators. Pezeshkian to Pakistani PM Sharif: US must remove blockade before talks resume.
